title: Reported Speech: He Said That...
description: Para contar lo que alguien dijo. Los tiempos verbales 'retroceden' (backshift). 'She said she was tired.' El discurso indirecto en inglés.

items.front: She said that she was feeling tired.
items.back: Ella dijo que se sentía cansada.
items.reading: /ʃiː sɛd ðæt ʃiː wəz ˈfiːlɪŋ ˈtaɪərd/
items.explanation: En reported speech los tiempos retroceden: present simple → past simple, present continuous → past continuous, will → would, can → could. También cambian pronombres (I → she) y expresiones de tiempo (today → that day, tomorrow → the next day).

items.front: He told me he had already eaten dinner.
items.back: Él me dijo que ya había cenado.
items.reading: /hiː toʊld miː hiː hæd ɔːlˈrɛdi ˈiːtən ˈdɪnər/
items.explanation: 'Say' vs 'tell': SAY no necesita objeto ('She said she was tired'). TELL necesita objeto indirecto ('She told ME she was tired'). Error muy común: 'She said me' está MAL. 'She told that' está MAL — tell siempre necesita a quién se le dice.

items.front: She asked me if I had ever been to Japan.
items.back: Ella me preguntó si alguna vez había estado en Japón.
items.reading: /ʃiː æskt miː ɪf aɪ hæd ˈɛvər bɪn tə dʒəˈpæn/
items.explanation: Para reportar preguntas: 'asked if/whether' (yes/no questions), o la palabra interrogativa (what, where, when...). El orden cambia a afirmativo — NO se usa inversión. CORRECTO: 'She asked where the station was.' INCORRECTO: 'She asked where was the station.'

steps.title: Reported Speech: el backshift de tiempos
steps.instruction: Cuando reportamos lo que alguien dijo, los tiempos verbales retroceden un paso. 'I am tired' → she said she WAS tired. 'I will help' → he said he WOULD help. El 'that' es opcional.

steps.title: Reported Questions: orden afirmativo
steps.instruction: Para preguntas indirectas, el orden de palabras cambia: becomes statement order. 'Where is the station?' → He asked WHERE the station WAS (no inversión).

steps.title: ¿Cuál es la forma correcta?
steps.instruction: Elegí la opción correcta de reported speech.

steps.title: Transformá al discurso indirecto
steps.instruction: Completá las oraciones de reported speech con el tiempo y forma correctos.

exercises.template: Direct: 'I love pizza.' → She said she {0} pizza.
Direct: 'I can swim.' → He told me he {0} swim.
Direct: 'Will you come?' → She asked me if I {0} come.
Direct: 'Where is the pharmacy?' → He asked where the pharmacy {0}.

steps.title: ¿Error o correcta?
steps.instruction: Encontrá los errores típicos de reported speech.

exercises.sentence: She said me that she was late.
exercises.correction: She told me that she was late. / She said (that) she was late.
exercises.explanation: INCORRECTO. 'Say' no necesita objeto indirecto: 'She said she was late.' 'Tell' SÍ necesita objeto: 'She TOLD ME she was late.' 'Said me' no existe en inglés.

exercises.sentence: He told his friends he would be late.
exercises.explanation: Correcto. 'Told + objeto (his friends) + reported clause'. 'Will' → 'would' en el backshift. Estructura perfecta.

exercises.sentence: She asked where was the nearest hospital.
exercises.correction: She asked where the nearest hospital was.
exercises.explanation: INCORRECTO. En reported questions, el orden de palabras es AFIRMATIVO, no de pregunta. No hay inversión. CORRECTO: 'She asked where the nearest hospital was' (sujeto + verbo, no verbo + sujeto).

exercises.sentence: They said they had finished the project.
exercises.explanation: Correcto. 'Said + reported clause' (sin objeto indirecto). 'Have finished' (present perfect) → 'had finished' (past perfect) en el backshift.

steps.title: ¡Lección completa!
steps.instruction: Reported Speech: los tiempos retroceden (present → past, will → would, can → could). SAY (sin objeto) vs TELL (con objeto: told me, told his friends). Para preguntas indirectas: asked if/whether (yes/no) o asked what/where/when + orden afirmativo (sin inversión). El 'that' es siempre opcional.
